newsletters.subject: The AI Cursor Arrives!
newsletters.reason: All 20 links are beeHiiv opaque redirects (link.mail.beehiiv.com). Cannot resolve destination URLs without browser. Subject 'The AI Cursor Arrives!' strongly suggests coverage of Cursor IDE (the AI coding editor), likely related to Cursor's 2.0 release push (per Pragmatic Engineer visit coverage), Wispr Flow, and the broader AI devtools ecosystem. Cursor has ~80 developers, 500K agent sessions in 30 days, and is a core wiki topic.
newsletters.wiki_relevance: HIGH - Cursor is already a major entity in the wiki. Any substantial article about Cursor 2.0, their AI workflows, or the competitive landscape with Claude Code/Codex is valuable.

newsletters.subject: Cerebras — Faster Tokens Please
newsletters.reason: SemiAnalysis deep-dive by Dylan Patel on Cerebras WSE-3 wafer-scale chip, CS-3 system, and the $24.6B OpenAI inference compute deal (750MW over 2026-2028). Covers fast inference economics, token pricing, BOM cost analysis, Cerebras IPO readiness, and the OpenAI-Cerebras MRA. Dylan Patel is already a documented entity in the wiki. No standalone Cerebras entity page exists yet — this is a major gap to fill.

newsletters.subject: [AINews] Codex Rises, Claude Meters Programmatic Usage
newsletters.reason: swyx's AINews digest (Latent.Space) covering major coding agent ecosystem developments: (1) OpenAI Codex surpassed 3M weekly active users (up from 2M a month ago), (2) Anthropic 'declawing' — every Claude subscription now gets a monthly credit of API tokens equal to the dollar amount of the plan ($200 sub = $200 API credits for programmatic usage), effectively metering third-party tool use, (3) OpenAI programmatic tool calling for Claude API, (4) the broader competitive landscape with Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex merging into a composable stack.

key_articles_identified.title: [AINews] Codex Rises, Claude Meters Programmatic Usage
Claude Code to Codex Migration Guide 2026
Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex are merging into one AI coding stack
